cmb-airport.short: Sri Lanka's main international gateway, sitting closer to Negombo than to Colombo itself

kandy.short: the sacred hill capital, where the Temple of the Tooth overlooks a calm central lake

sigiriya.short: home to the Lion Rock fortress, with Dambulla's cave temples a short drive away in the island's Cultural Triangle

galle.short: a 17th-century Dutch fort town of ramparts, a lighthouse and boutique-lined lanes on the south coast

mirissa.short: a laid-back south-coast beach known for whale watching, surf and palm-fringed sunsets

ella.short: a small hill town of tea slopes, the Nine Arch Bridge and hikes up Little Adam's Peak

negombo.short: a lagoon-and-beach town with a lively fish market, just minutes from the international airport

colombo.short: Sri Lanka's commercial capital, mixing the colonial-era Fort district with Galle Face Green

nuwara-eliya.short: a misty tea-country town nicknamed 'Little England', perched at nearly 1,900 metres

yala.short: Sri Lanka's best-known safari park, famed for one of the world's densest leopard populations

arugam-bay.short: a mellow east-coast surf town built around a legendary right-hand point break

cmb-airport|kandy.intro: Just under three hours that climb from sea-level coconut groves to Kandy's lake: your driver meets you in arrivals and heads inland via Kegalle, with Pinnawala's elephants an easy stop en route. Go private for door-to-door ease, or take a cheaper seat on the daily shared shuttle into the Cultural Triangle.
highlights: Pinnawala's elephants, an easy detour near Kegalle
Bible Rock views at the Kadugannawa pass
Temple of the Sacred Tooth Relic beside Kandy Lake
Royal Botanic Gardens at Peradeniya, just before town
cmb-airport|kandy.back: Downhill nearly all the way: leave the lake behind, drop through the Kadugannawa pass and let your driver time the run to Katunayake around the Kegalle-road traffic. Works well for evening departures.

cmb-airport|sigiriya.intro: Straight from the arrivals hall into the Cultural Triangle: the road runs through Kurunegala's coconut country before opening onto dry-zone plains, with Dambulla's cave temples a natural first stop before Sigiriya rock rises ahead. Book private for stops on demand, or split the cost on the daily shared run into the Cultural Triangle.
highlights: Dambulla's painted cave temples, right on the route
Sigiriya rock rising from the dry-zone plains
Coconut-triangle scenery around Kurunegala
Pidurangala rock for a sunrise climb
Seasonal elephant gatherings at Minneriya nearby
cmb-airport|sigiriya.back: Leave after a sunrise climb of Pidurangala and still make an afternoon flight: the run back through Kurunegala takes a shade under three and a half hours, with your driver watching the clock so you don't have to.

cmb-airport|galle.intro: From touchdown to the ramparts in a little over two hours: this run links the airport expressway straight onto the Southern Expressway, skirting Colombo entirely. You could watch the sun drop behind Galle Fort's lighthouse the same evening you land. Private car only on this route — door to door, no changes.
highlights: Galle Fort's Dutch ramparts and lighthouse
Expressway all the way — Colombo never slows you down
Unawatuna's swimming beach, ten minutes further on
Boutique cafés in the fort's old merchant streets
cmb-airport|galle.back: Galle to the departure gate without touching Colombo: join the Southern Expressway at the edge of town and you're at Katunayake in around two and a quarter hours. Perfect after one last morning on the ramparts.

cmb-airport|mirissa.intro: Blue whales don't wait, and neither should you: the expressway carries you past Colombo and down the south coast in around two and a half hours. Break the journey at Galle Fort if you fancy, then roll into Mirissa for sunset at Coconut Tree Hill. Private transfers only on this run — your driver, your schedule.
highlights: Whale-watching boats out of Mirissa harbour (November to April)
Sunset from Coconut Tree Hill
Optional break at Galle Fort on the way down
Weligama's long surf beach, five minutes next door
cmb-airport|mirissa.back: The dawn-flight special: Mirissa to the check-in desk in around two and a half hours, nearly all of it expressway. Book a night pickup and doze in the back while your driver takes the empty roads.

cmb-airport|ella.intro: The long haul, done properly: about five hours from the arrivals hall to the cool air of the hill country. The Southern Expressway eats the first stretch; the climb through Wellawaya and the Ella Gap, past Rawana Falls, rewards the rest. A private car lets you set the pace, with lunch and photo stops built in.
highlights: Rawana Falls, right beside the road on the final climb
The Ella Gap — the view that says you've arrived
Buduruwagala's rock-cut Buddhas, a short detour at Wellawaya
Nine Arch Bridge and Little Adam's Peak once you're in
cmb-airport|ella.back: Downhill through the Ella Gap, then expressway the rest of the way to Katunayake — about five hours door to door. Leave after breakfast for an evening flight and keep a small buffer for hill-road traffic.

cmb-airport|negombo.intro: Seven kilometres is all that separates the baggage carousel from Negombo's beach hotels — about twenty minutes door to door — which is why the town makes an ideal first or last night in Sri Lanka. Go private for a driver waiting at arrivals, or hop on the daily shared service that covers this short leg too.
highlights: Negombo's long beach strip and lagoon-crab dinners
Lellama fish market and its outrigger fleet
Dutch-era canal winding through town
St Mary's Church and Negombo's Catholic quarter
cmb-airport|negombo.back: The classic last-night move: sleep beside the beach, eat lagoon crab, and be at check-in twenty minutes after hotel pickup. Drivers run this hop at all hours for red-eye departures.

cmb-airport|colombo.intro: Colombo traffic has a fearsome reputation, but the airport expressway sidesteps the worst of it: 35 kilometres, across the Kelani River and into the city, usually inside the hour. Your driver waits at arrivals and drops you at the hotel door; a cheaper daily shared seat also departs the airport.
highlights: Galle Face Green for a sunset stroll
Gangaramaya Temple beside Beira Lake
Pettah's bazaars and the old Fort district
Lotus Tower on the skyline as you cross the Kelani
cmb-airport|colombo.back: From Fort or Kollupitiya to Katunayake in under an hour once you're on the expressway. Your driver pads the pickup time around Colombo's rush hour, so early flights are never a squeeze.

negombo|sigiriya.intro: Skip Colombo entirely: from Negombo the road runs inland through coconut country and Kurunegala, putting you under Sigiriya's rock in a little over three hours. Go private for door-to-door comfort and a stop at Dambulla's cave temples, or take a seat on the daily shared ride from the airport into the Cultural Triangle.
highlights: Coconut Triangle plantations around Giriulla and Kurunegala
Athugala, the elephant-shaped rock above Kurunegala
Dambulla's UNESCO-listed cave temples en route
Sigiriya rising sheer from the plains
Minneriya's elephants a short drive beyond
negombo|sigiriya.back: Sigiriya to Negombo makes a tidy last leg before a flight: 148 km, about 3 hours 15 minutes, with time for Dambulla's caves on the way out if you leave after breakfast.

negombo|kandy.intro: Beach town to hill capital in under three hours: leaving Negombo, lagoon and palms give way to rubber and spice country around Kegalle before the road climbs the Kadugannawa pass into Kandy. A private car lets you detour to Pinnawala's elephants; a cheaper daily shared seat from the airport covers this run too.
highlights: Pinnawala Elephant Orphanage detour near Kegalle
Kadugannawa pass with views of Bible Rock
Spice gardens around Mawanella
Royal Botanic Gardens at Peradeniya
Kandy Lake and the Temple of the Sacred Tooth
negombo|kandy.back: Kandy to Negombo is the classic pre-flight run — about 115 km and 2 hours 50 minutes down through the Kadugannawa hairpins, door to door, with room for a Pinnawala stop if you set off early.

colombo|kandy.intro: Travellers have taken the Colombo–Kandy road since colonial days, past the cashew stalls of Cadjugama, the old rest house at Ambepussa and the hairpin climb at Kadugannawa. A private transfer stops wherever you fancy; a cheaper daily shared seat, departing from the airport, makes the same climb.
highlights: Cadjugama's roadside cashew stalls
Utuwankanda, the rock hideout of folk outlaw Saradiel
Bible Rock views on the Kadugannawa climb
Royal Botanic Gardens at Peradeniya
Temple of the Sacred Tooth beside Kandy Lake
colombo|kandy.back: Downhill from Kandy, the 123 km back to Colombo takes about three and a half hours with traffic. A door-to-door car beats hauling luggage to the station, and you can time departure around your checkout.

colombo|galle.intro: Two roads lead south from Colombo: the Southern Expressway reaches Galle Fort in about two and a quarter hours, while the old coast road strings together Kalutara, Bentota and Hikkaduwa. A private transfer lets you mix both — motorway out, then a turtle hatchery or beach stop wherever the mood takes you.
highlights: Galle Fort's UNESCO-listed ramparts and lighthouse
Kosgoda turtle hatcheries on the coast-road option
Madu River boat safari near Balapitiya
Bentota beach and river mouth
Kalutara Bodhiya rising above the Kalu Ganga
colombo|galle.back: Galle to Colombo is an easy 133 km expressway run of around 2 hours 15 minutes — or trade speed for the coast road through Hikkaduwa and Bentota. Door-to-door timing suits early flights.

colombo|ella.intro: At 314 km, Colombo to Ella is the long haul worth doing in comfort: flat, fast motorway south, then a slow, spectacular climb from Wellawaya past Rawana Falls into the Ella Gap. A private car means your own schedule — and a detour to Buduruwagala's giant rock-cut Buddhas that buses never make.
highlights: Buduruwagala's colossal rock-cut Buddhas near Wellawaya
Rawana Falls beside the final climb
The Ella Gap opening up as you gain height
Nine Arch Bridge and Little Adam's Peak on arrival
colombo|ella.back: Leaving the hills, Ella to Colombo takes close to five hours — the Ella Gap descent past Rawana Falls first, then quick lowland roads home. A door-to-door private transfer spares you the bus-and-train shuffle with luggage.

sigiriya|kandy.intro: From Sigiriya's rock plains the A9 winds south through Matale's spice country — 89 km and about two and a half hours to Kandy. A private car gives you door-to-door comfort and stops at Dambulla's cave temples or Nalanda Gedige; a daily Airport–Cultural Triangle shared ride is the cheaper option.
highlights: Dambulla's painted cave temples, just off the road
Nalanda Gedige, an ancient stone shrine at the island's centre
Spice gardens around Matale for cinnamon and pepper
Aluvihare Rock Temple near Matale
Kandy Lake and the Temple of the Sacred Tooth
sigiriya|kandy.back: Northbound, you trade Kandy's hill air for dry-zone plains: down past Matale and through Dambulla's junction bustle, with Sigiriya's rock rising ahead in the final half hour.

kandy|ella.intro: This is the full hill-country traverse: 136 km of tea, waterfalls and hairpins, close to four hours by road. Your driver can pause at a working tea factory — or drop you at the station to ride the celebrated blue train for the final leg. A daily Kandy–Hill Country shared seat is the cheaper way to go.
highlights: Ramboda Falls tumbling beside the A5
Tea factory tours between Pussellawa and Nuwara Eliya
Option to join the famous hill train for the last stretch
Ella Gap views on the hill road
Nine Arch Bridge a short tuk-tuk ride from Ella
kandy|ella.back: Ella to Kandy reverses the climb: out through the Gap, up past Nuwara Eliya's tea terraces, then the long green descent to the lake city. Around four hours, with waterfall stops on request.

kandy|nuwara-eliya.intro: Tea country begins almost as soon as you leave Kandy: the A5 corkscrews up through Gampola and Pussellawa for 76 km, about two and a half hours of estates and cooling air. A private car can pull over at Ramboda Falls or a factory tasting; a daily shared ride to the hill country also covers this leg.
highlights: Ramboda Falls and its roadside viewpoint
Tea estates stacked above the road at Pussellawa
Factory tastings at working estates along the A5
Gregory Lake and Victoria Park in Nuwara Eliya
Nuwara Eliya's 'Little England' post office and racecourse
kandy|nuwara-eliya.back: Downhill from Little England, the return to Kandy unwinds the same hairpins in around two and a half hours — mist lifting off the valleys early on, tea pluckers at work beside the road.

nuwara-eliya|ella.intro: The shortest hop in the hills, and one of the prettiest: 54 km, just under two hours, dropping from Nuwara Eliya's cool plateau past Hakgala and the vegetable terraces of Welimada into Ella's warmer air. A private car stops wherever you like; a daily shared hill-country seat is the budget option.
highlights: Seetha Amman Temple, tied to the Ramayana legend
Hakgala Botanical Garden clinging to the mountainside
Welimada's patchwork of vegetable farms
Ella Gap views on the mountain road
Rawana Falls a short ride from Ella
nuwara-eliya|ella.back: Climbing from Ella back to Nuwara Eliya takes just under two hours: through Welimada's farmland, past Hakgala's gardens, arriving as the temperature drops and the pines take over.

ella|yala.intro: Leave the tea country at the Ella Gap and watch the road spill downhill through hairpins into hot dry-zone plains — 126 km, about 3 hours 18 minutes. A private car can pause at Rawana Falls or Buduruwagala's rock Buddhas en route; a cheaper shared seat heads to Yala daily at 8:00am.
highlights: Rawana Falls, roadside on the descent from Ella
Buduruwagala's rock-cut Buddhas near Wellawaya
Tissamaharama's lake and white dagoba
Safari jeeps queueing at the Yala park gates
ella|yala.back: Coming out of the park, the road climbs from Tissamaharama through Wellawaya and up the Ella Gap, trading safari dust for tea-country cool over roughly 3 hours 18 minutes.

ella|arugam-bay.intro: Breakfast in the hills, surf by mid-afternoon: 134 km and about 3 hours separate Ella from Arugam Bay, dropping through Wellawaya and Monaragala to the east coast. Private cars carry boards and stop on demand; a daily 8:00am shared ride heads east via Yala.
highlights: Wild elephants grazing near the road at Lahugala
Monaragala's paddy and rubber country
Muhudu Maha Viharaya, the beachside temple at Pottuvil
Main Point, Arugam Bay's famous right-hand break
ella|arugam-bay.back: Westbound from the point breaks, the road runs past Pottuvil's lagoon and Lahugala's elephant country before climbing through Wellawaya into Ella's cool green gap — about 3 hours.

ella|mirissa.intro: Ella to Mirissa trades thousand-metre tea country for a palm-backed bay: the road drops to Wellawaya, then runs fast across the southern plains — 173 km, about 3 hours — to Mirissa's sweep of sand. Go private to break the trip where you fancy, or take the daily 8:30am shared ride to the south coast.
highlights: Rawana Falls in the Ella Gap
Buffalo-curd and treacle stalls along the southern plains
Coconut Tree Hill at the eastern end of Mirissa beach
Blue-whale boats leaving Mirissa harbour at dawn (November to April)
ella|mirissa.back: The reverse run swaps sea level for thousand-metre air: across the plains to Wellawaya, then up the hairpins of the Ella Gap — beach towel to jumper in around 3 hours.

yala|mirissa.intro: This one hugs the coast almost the whole way: 155 km and about 3 hours 17 minutes from the lodges around Tissamaharama past Hambantota's salt pans, Tangalle's coves and Dondra's lighthouse to Mirissa. A private car can detour to the Hummanaya blowhole; the shared ride south leaves daily at 8:00am.
highlights: Lagoons and salt pans around Bundala, busy with birdlife
Tangalle's quiet crescent beaches
Hummanaya blowhole near Kudawella
Dondra Head lighthouse at the island's southernmost point
Mirissa's whale-watching harbour
yala|mirissa.back: Eastbound, the shoreline unwinds in reverse — Matara, Dickwella, Tangalle — before the road turns inland at Hambantota for Tissamaharama, timed nicely for a next-morning Yala safari.

mirissa|galle.intro: At 41 km and under an hour, this is less a transfer than a slow reveal of the south coast: Weligama's stilt-fishing posts, Koggala's island-dotted lake, Unawatuna's curve of sand, then Galle Fort's Dutch ramparts. Private cars run door to door with stops; a shared coastal shuttle runs twice daily.
highlights: Stilt-fishing posts in the shallows off Weligama and Ahangama
Koggala Lake and its island temples
Unawatuna beach and the Peace Pagoda on Rumassala
Galle Fort's 17th-century ramparts and lighthouse
mirissa|galle.back: From the fort gates it's under an hour east along the shore, past Unawatuna and Weligama's surf schools to Mirissa — easy to time around a dawn whale-watching boat.

galle|ella.intro: Two Sri Lankas in one afternoon. From Galle's ramparts the expressway carries you across the deep south, then the road turns inland near Hambantota, crosses dry scrubland to Wellawaya and climbs the Ella Gap — 198 km in about 3 hours 18 minutes. No shared ride runs here, so a door-to-door private car is the way.
highlights: A fast expressway leg across the deep south
Buduruwagala's seven rock-cut figures, a short detour near Wellawaya
Rawana Falls in the Ella Gap
Ella Gap hairpins with views back over the plains
galle|ella.back: It's a long descent to the coast: out through the Ella Gap, across the plains to the expressway, and into Galle in time for sunset on the ramparts — about 3 hours 18 minutes.
